paper_title: AgentSpec: Customizable Runtime Enforcement for Safe and Reliable LLM Agents
rule_description: Financial transfer to non-verified family member requires explicit user confirmation.
normalized_rule: Do not execute Transfer unless the recipient is a verified family member or explicit prior user approval exists.

paper_title: AgentSpec: Customizable Runtime Enforcement for Safe and Reliable LLM Agents
rule_description: Python code that requests content from an untrusted source and writes or prints it requires user inspection.
normalized_rule: Do not execute PythonREPL code that consumes an untrusted source and writes, prints, or outputs the content unless user inspection approves it first.

paper_title: AgentSpec: Customizable Runtime Enforcement for Safe and Reliable LLM Agents
rule_description: Embodied agent must not pour liquid into an object that should not get wet.
normalized_rule: Do not execute pour when target_object.wettable=false; stop before physical hazard occurs.

paper_title: AgentSpec: Customizable Runtime Enforcement for Safe and Reliable LLM Agents
rule_description: Autonomous driving agent must correct behavior when front vehicle is too close.
normalized_rule: When front_vehicle_distance<10 during state_change, enforce corrective control before continuing the trajectory.
notes: P01 Rule 4; state invariant with corrective action.

paper_title: GUARDIAN: Safeguarding LLM Multi-Agent Collaborations with Temporal Graph Modeling
rule_description: Hallucinated or anomalous agent response must be removed before it propagates downstream.
normalized_rule: Remove anomalous agent response nodes and outgoing propagation paths before subsequent collaboration rounds.

paper_title: GUARDIAN: Safeguarding LLM Multi-Agent Collaborations with Temporal Graph Modeling
rule_description: Compromised agent node with high attribute anomaly must be excluded before others consume its output.
normalized_rule: Exclude an agent node from later graph iterations when its response-attribute reconstruction error or anomaly score is high.
notes: P02 Rule 2; governance action must occur before downstream use.

paper_title: GUARDIAN: Safeguarding LLM Multi-Agent Collaborations with Temporal Graph Modeling
rule_description: Corrupted inter-agent communication edge must be pruned or the affected node excluded.
normalized_rule: Remove anomalous communication edges or affected nodes when edge-level corruption is detected before the corrupted information propagates.

paper_title: GUARDIAN: Safeguarding LLM Multi-Agent Collaborations with Temporal Graph Modeling
rule_description: Temporal graph compression must preserve anomaly-relevant temporal, structural, and attribute evidence.
normalized_rule: Use a compressed temporal interaction graph only if task-relevant node attributes, edge structure, and temporal dependencies required for anomaly detection are preserved.
notes: P02 Rule 4; direct fit for preservation check.

paper_title: AgentBound: Securing Execution Boundaries of AI Agents
rule_description: MCP server resource access is default-deny unless declared in manifest and granted at runtime.
normalized_rule: Block MCP resource access unless the capability appears in AgentManifest and a concrete runtime permission with user consent exists.
condition: requested_capability NOT_IN manifest.capabilities OR concrete_target NOT_IN runtime_permissions

paper_title: AgentBound: Securing Execution Boundaries of AI Agents
rule_description: Environment variable reads require declared env-read capability and variable-specific runtime permission.
normalized_rule: Do not read environment variables unless mcp.ac.system.env.read is declared and the exact variable is whitelisted by runtime permission.

paper_title: AgentBound: Securing Execution Boundaries of AI Agents
rule_description: Outbound network traffic must be restricted to manifest/user-approved endpoints.
normalized_rule: Block network_connect when the destination host, IP, or URL is outside the allowed endpoint set derived from manifest and runtime permission.

paper_title: AgentBound: Securing Execution Boundaries of AI Agents
rule_description: Filesystem access must be scoped by approved path and access mode.
normalized_rule: Allow file read/write/delete only when the path is inside approved scope and the requested access mode is covered by granted permission.

paper_title: AgentBound: Securing Execution Boundaries of AI Agents
rule_description: OS command execution requires explicit system-exec capability and approved command scope.
normalized_rule: Block subprocess, shell, or CLI execution unless mcp.ac.system.exec is declared and the concrete command is approved by runtime permission.

paper_title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems
rule_description: Untrusted data must not be mixed with privileged context without isolation, labeling, or sanitization.
normalized_rule: Before untrusted input or tool output influences privileged tool calls, require isolation, provenance labeling, or sanitization.
notes: P04 Rule 1; context-integrity and process-isolation rule.

paper_title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems
rule_description: Tool invocation must use immutable trusted tool identity and validated arguments.
normalized_rule: Block tool invocation if tool provenance is missing, implementation path has changed, tool is unregistered, or arguments contain command injection.
condition: tool_unregistered OR tool_path_changed OR argument_integrity_failed OR provenance_missing

paper_title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems
rule_description: Executable skills and tools must run inside a sandbox that mediates filesystem, network, environment, and credentials.
normalized_rule: Deny skill/tool execution or resource access when requested effects exceed the sandbox scope or escape a mediated environment.

paper_title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems
rule_description: Security logs and audit records must not be overwritten, deleted, or truncated by arbitrary tools or skills.
normalized_rule: Allow log writes only from trusted runtime logger in append-only mode; block delete, truncate, or overwrite and raise tampering verdict.
notes: P04 Rule 6; audit integrity as governance evidence.

paper_title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
rule_description: Filesystem access requires a scoped FileSystem capability, approved root, and non-escaping handles.
normalized_rule: Reject or block filesystem access if FileSystem capability is missing, path is outside approved root, or FileEntry/FileSystem handle escapes capability scope.

paper_title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
rule_description: Classified data transformations must be pure and cannot capture side-effect capabilities.
normalized_rule: Reject Classified.map/flatMap transformations when the closure captures IO, filesystem, network, process, or other effect capabilities.

paper_title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
rule_description: Classified values must be redacted on agent-visible output channels.
normalized_rule: If value type is Classified[T] and output channel is observable by the cloud agent, emit only redacted representation; plaintext is allowed only through secure user-only channel.

paper_title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
rule_description: Network access requires an active Network capability and destination host allowlist.
normalized_rule: Block httpGet/httpPost unless a scoped Network capability is active and URL host is in allowedHosts.

paper_title: Make Agent Defeat Agent: Automatic Detection of Taint-Style Vulnerabilities in LLM-based Agents
rule_description: Prompt-derived or LLM-derived data must not reach security-sensitive sinks without sanitization.
normalized_rule: Report violation when tainted data from user prompt or LLM response flows into a security-sensitive sink argument without adequate sanitizer or validator on the path.

paper_title: Make Agent Defeat Agent: Automatic Detection of Taint-Style Vulnerabilities in LLM-based Agents
rule_description: Security-sensitive sinks must be explicitly classified and monitored.
normalized_rule: When a call matches a predefined sensitive sink signature, require sink-specific validation and taint evidence; otherwise mark the sink call high-risk or unknown.
backend_candidate: CodeQL sink detector/static registry/Python audit hook/sys.settrace
notes: P06 Rule 2; covers code, command, SQL, SSRF, and template sinks.

paper_title: Make Agent Defeat Agent: Automatic Detection of Taint-Style Vulnerabilities in LLM-based Agents
rule_description: LLM-selected tool/component needs provenance from prompt and response to runtime call chain.
normalized_rule: If the LLM selects a component whose call chain reaches a sink, record prompt, LLM response, parsed action, and call-chain provenance; otherwise evidence is incomplete.

paper_title: Make Agent Defeat Agent: Automatic Detection of Taint-Style Vulnerabilities in LLM-based Agents
rule_description: Stored prompt payloads can create second-order vulnerabilities when later retrieved into sinks.
normalized_rule: Preserve cross-turn taint provenance from prompt-derived stored values to later sink arguments; violation if retrieved taint reaches a sink unsanitized.
trigger: event_type=state_write FROM prompt_or_llm_output AND later event_type=state_read_to_sink
backend_candidate: Stateful taint tracker/LTL/replay monitor/temporal database verifier
